text.title: ADR Consequence Evidence Binding Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the adr-consequence-evidence-binding methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale. The terminal `skip-this-methodology` rule is the safety valve invoked by the decision tree when preconditions fail.

rule.statement: Each item in `## Consequences` MUST cite ≥1 evidence anchor (CI fitness script, KPI dashboard, log query).
rule.rationale: Without an anchor consequences cannot be re-evaluated.

rule.statement: Anchor MUST be a URL or file path the build system can resolve, not a prose description.
rule.rationale: Prose anchors do not survive search + automation.

rule.statement: Frontmatter MUST set `next_review` ≤ 6 months from decision date.
When superseded, the new ADR MUST link back to the original with `supersedes: ADR-NNNN`.
rule.rationale: Lost links break the decision trail.

rule.statement: Each measurable consequence MUST name a numeric threshold + direction (e.g. `p95 latency < 200ms`).
rule.rationale: Without a threshold the evidence is a graph, not a verdict.

rule.statement: Author MUST stop and route elsewhere when the decision-tree resolves to a skip leaf; partial application of this methodology against an unfit input is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Forcing the methodology onto a mismatched input produces low-quality artefacts and erodes trust in the corpus.
